title: monardella viridis subsp. saxicola
intro: Monardella viridis subsp. saxicola, commonly known as San Jacinto Mountain monardella, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family that is endemic to San Jacinto Mountain in Riverside County, California. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Monardella viridis subsp. saxicola are attributed to its essential oils, which have been used to aid in the relief of colds, sore throats, and coughs, as well as to reduce fever and inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas and tinctures, can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water or extracting the essential oils using solvents like ethanol or glycerin.
